title: 1351. Lakshadweep is a Union Territory of India located in which sea?
hint: It is located southwest of the Indian mainland.
summary: Lakshadweep islands are located in the Arabian Sea.

title: 1352. What is the capital of Lakshadweep Union Territory?
hint: It is located on Kavaratti Island.
summary: Kavaratti is the capital and largest inhabited island of Lakshadweep.

title: 1353. Lakshadweep islands are mainly of which geological origin?
hint: They are associated with coral formations.
summary: Lakshadweep islands are coral atolls formed mainly from coral reefs.

title: 1354. Which is the largest island in the Lakshadweep group by area?
hint: It is located in the southern part of Lakshadweep.
summary: Andrott Island is the largest island of Lakshadweep by area.

title: 1355. How many islands are there in the Lakshadweep archipelago?
hint: This is the official number of islands in the Union Territory.
summary: Lakshadweep consists of 36 islands, including inhabited and uninhabited islands.

title: 1356. Which island of Lakshadweep is closest to the Maldives?
hint: It is the southernmost island of Lakshadweep.
summary: Minicoy Island is the southernmost island of Lakshadweep and is geographically close to the Maldives.

title: 1357. Which language is predominantly spoken by the people of Lakshadweep?
hint: It is closely related to the language spoken in Kerala.
summary: Malayalam is the most widely spoken language in Lakshadweep, while Mahl is spoken in Minicoy.

title: 1358. Which type of ecosystem is mainly found around Lakshadweep islands?
hint: It supports marine biodiversity and protects coastlines.
summary: Coral reef ecosystems are the dominant marine ecosystems around Lakshadweep.

title: 1359. Lakshadweep was formed as a Union Territory in which year?
hint: It was separated from Madras State.
summary: Lakshadweep became a Union Territory in 1956 under the States Reorganisation Act.

title: 1360. Which is the largest inhabited island of Lakshadweep?
hint: It is also the administrative capital of Lakshadweep.
summary: Kavaratti is the largest inhabited island and administrative headquarters of Lakshadweep.

title: 1361. Lakshadweep is the smallest Union Territory of India in terms of:
hint: Consider geographical size.
summary: Lakshadweep is the smallest Union Territory of India by area.

title: 1362. Which island of Lakshadweep has an airport?
hint: It serves as the main air gateway to Lakshadweep.
summary: Agatti Island has the only airport in Lakshadweep.

title: 1363. Which island group of India is famous for coral atolls in the Arabian Sea?
hint: It is a Union Territory located near Kerala.
summary: Lakshadweep is known for coral atolls in the Arabian Sea.

title: 1364. Which water body separates Lakshadweep from the Indian mainland?
hint: The islands lie southwest of Kerala.
summary: The Arabian Sea separates Lakshadweep from mainland India.

title: 1365. Which of the following is NOT an inhabited island of Lakshadweep?
hint: It is a coral island without permanent inhabitants.
summary: Kalpeni, Kavaratti, Minicoy and Agatti are inhabited islands; some islands remain uninhabited.

title: 1366. Lakshadweep administration comes under which ministry of the Government of India?
hint: This ministry handles Union Territories.
summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs administers Union Territories including Lakshadweep.

title: 1367. Which traditional dance form is associated with Lakshadweep?
hint: It is a folk dance performed by men.
summary: Lava dance is a traditional folk dance of Lakshadweep.

title: 1368. Which island is the administrative headquarters of Lakshadweep?
hint: It is also the capital of the Union Territory.
summary: Kavaratti is the administrative headquarters of Lakshadweep.

title: 1369. The Lakshadweep islands are part of which larger island chain?
hint: They are coral islands of the Indian Ocean region.
summary: Lakshadweep forms part of the Laccadive, Minicoy and Amindivi group.

title: 1370. Which marine organism is mainly responsible for the formation of coral reefs in Lakshadweep?
hint: They are tiny marine animals living in colonies.
summary: Coral polyps are responsible for building coral reefs.

title: 1371. Which of the following is a group of coral islands in Lakshadweep?
hint: It includes Laccadive, Minicoy and Amindivi islands.
summary: Lakshadweep is a group of coral islands in the Arabian Sea.

title: 1372. Which island is famous for the Minicoy Lighthouse in Lakshadweep?
hint: It is the southernmost inhabited island of Lakshadweep.
summary: Minicoy Island is famous for its historic lighthouse.

title: 1373. Which type of reef structure is commonly found around Lakshadweep islands?
hint: It is a ring-shaped coral reef.
summary: Atolls are the dominant reef structures found around Lakshadweep.

title: 1374. Which district structure exists in Lakshadweep Union Territory?
hint: The entire Union Territory is administered as a single district.
summary: Lakshadweep has one district administration.

title: 1375. Which island of Lakshadweep is known for tuna fishing?
hint: It is one of the major inhabited islands.
summary: Minicoy and other Lakshadweep islands have a strong tuna fishing tradition.

title: 1376. Which sea route connects Lakshadweep with mainland India?
hint: Ships operate from Kerala ports.
summary: Lakshadweep is connected with Kerala ports through Arabian Sea routes.

title: 1377. Which of the following is a famous tourist destination in Lakshadweep?
hint: It is known for lagoons and water sports.
summary: Bangaram Island is a popular tourist destination in Lakshadweep.

title: 1378. Which Indian state is geographically closest to Lakshadweep?
hint: It lies on the southwest coast of India.
summary: Kerala is the nearest Indian state to Lakshadweep.

title: 1379. Which oceanic feature surrounds many Lakshadweep islands?
hint: It is a shallow water body enclosed by coral reefs.
summary: Lagoons are common features around Lakshadweep coral islands.

title: 1380. Which sector is a major contributor to the economy of Lakshadweep?
hint: It includes fishing activities.
summary: Fishing and tourism are important economic activities in Lakshadweep.

title: 1381. Lakshadweep is separated from the Maldives by which channel?
hint: It is named after the southernmost island of Lakshadweep.
summary: The Eight Degree Channel separates Minicoy Island from the Maldives.

title: 1382. Which island group forms the northern part of Lakshadweep?
hint: It includes islands such as Amini and Chetlat.
summary: The Amindivi Islands form the northern part of Lakshadweep.

title: 1383. Which of the following islands belongs to Lakshadweep?
hint: It is one of the inhabited islands.
summary: Kalpeni is one of the inhabited islands of Lakshadweep.

title: 1384. What is the official language used for administration in Lakshadweep?
hint: It is one of the official languages of India.
summary: Malayalam is widely used for administration in Lakshadweep, along with English.

title: 1385. Which of the following is a major environmental concern for Lakshadweep?
hint: It affects coral ecosystems.
summary: Coral bleaching due to climate change and rising sea temperatures is a major concern.

title: 1386. Which island is included in the Laccadive group of Lakshadweep?
hint: It is one of the major islands near Kavaratti.
summary: Agatti is part of the Laccadive group of islands.

title: 1387. Which activity attracts tourists to Lakshadweep?
hint: It is related to marine recreation.
summary: Scuba diving and water sports are popular tourism activities in Lakshadweep.

title: 1388. Which constitutional article deals with the administration of Union Territories?
hint: It is related to Union Territories.
summary: Article 239 deals with administration of Union Territories.

title: 1389. Which crop is commonly cultivated in Lakshadweep?
hint: It is widely grown in tropical coastal regions.
summary: Coconut is an important crop of Lakshadweep.

title: 1390. Lakshadweep has which type of climate?
hint: It is influenced by the surrounding sea.
summary: Lakshadweep has a tropical maritime climate.

title: 1391. Which island of Lakshadweep is known as the gateway to the islands because of its airport?
hint: It has the only airport in Lakshadweep.
summary: Agatti Island is considered the gateway to Lakshadweep due to its airport.

title: 1392. Which group of islands forms the southern part of Lakshadweep?
hint: It includes Minicoy Island.
summary: The Laccadive group and Minicoy form the southern region of Lakshadweep.

title: 1393. Which marine resource is important for the economy of Lakshadweep?
hint: It is a major fishery product.
summary: Tuna fishing is an important economic activity in Lakshadweep.

title: 1394. Lakshadweep has which type of islands?
hint: They are formed from coral deposits.
summary: Lakshadweep consists mainly of coral islands.

title: 1395. Which city in Kerala is the main entry point for ships going to Lakshadweep?
hint: It is a major port city of Kerala.
summary: Kochi is the main gateway port for Lakshadweep.

title: 1396. Which protected area helps conserve marine biodiversity around Lakshadweep?
hint: It protects coral reefs and marine life.
summary: Marine protected areas in Lakshadweep help conserve coral reef ecosystems.

title: 1397. Which island is the capital of Lakshadweep Union Territory?
hint: It is also the administrative headquarters.
summary: Kavaratti is the capital of Lakshadweep.

title: 1398. Which traditional food ingredient is widely produced in Lakshadweep?
hint: It comes from coconut trees.
summary: Coconut products are important in Lakshadweep.

title: 1399. Which channel separates Kavaratti and Agatti islands in Lakshadweep?
hint: It is named after the degree of latitude.
summary: The Nine Degree Channel separates Kavaratti from Kalpeni and the islands north of it.

title: 1400. Which factor makes Lakshadweep vulnerable to climate change?
hint: The islands are very low-lying.
summary: Low elevation and sea-level rise pose major risks to Lakshadweep.
